---
title: "References and Citation Strategy"
nocite: |
  @*
---

## Citation Strategy

The course cites sources for four distinct purposes:

1. **Foundations** for probability, Bayesian theory, and decision theory.
2. **Methodological landmarks** for Monte Carlo, MCMC, HMC, variational inference, and Bayesian workflow.
3. **Modeling references** for hierarchical models, Gaussian processes, nonparametrics, and predictive evaluation.
4. **Software references** for `R`, `Stan`, `cmdstanr`, `posterior`, `bayesplot`, `loo`, and `JAX`.

## Linked Reference Guide

The bibliography below remains the formal citation record. This section is the practical reading guide: each item links to a publisher page, official documentation page, DOI landing page, or open-access version where possible.

## Annotated Reading Guide By Course Part

### Part I. Foundations

- [Foundations of the Theory of Probability](https://store.doverpublications.com/products/9780486601595)  
  Formal probability foundations and the language behind measurable probability models.
- [Probability: Theory and Examples](https://bookstore.ams.org/amstext-49/)  
  A strong reference for conditioning, convergence, and rigorous probability arguments.
- [Bayesian Theory](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/book/10.1002/9780470316870)  
  Foundational Bayesian reasoning, decision structure, and posterior logic.

### Part II. Core Bayesian Inference

- [Bayesian Data Analysis](https://www.routledge.com/Bayesian-Data-Analysis-Third-Edition/Gelman-Carlin-Stern-Dunson-Vehtari-Rubin/p/book/9781439840955)  
  The main all-purpose reference for posterior inference, priors, prediction, and applied Bayesian workflow.
- [Statistical Decision Theory and Bayesian Analysis](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-1-4757-4286-2)  
  Canonical text for loss, utility, Bayes rules, and decision-theoretic structure.
- [Prior Distributions for Variance Parameters in Hierarchical Models](https://doi.org/10.1214/06-BA117A)  
  Influential discussion of weakly informative prior design.

### Part III. Computation

- [Monte Carlo Statistical Methods](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-1-4757-4145-2)  
  Core reference for Monte Carlo, importance sampling, MCMC, and asymptotic simulation logic.
- [The No-U-Turn Sampler](https://jmlr.org/papers/v15/hoffman14a.html)  
  Canonical NUTS paper, central for modern Stan computation.
- [A Conceptual Introduction to Hamiltonian Monte Carlo](https://arxiv.org/abs/1701.02434)  
  Best geometry-first explanation of HMC pathologies and behavior.
- [Variational Inference: A Review for Statisticians](https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.2017.1285773)  
  Clear overview of ELBOs, KL geometry, and variational approximations.

### Part IV. Models

- [Gaussian Processes for Machine Learning](https://gaussianprocess.org/gpml/)  
  Standard reference for kernel-based Bayesian function modeling.
- [A Bayesian Analysis of Some Nonparametric Problems](https://doi.org/10.1214/aos/1176342360)  
  Foundational Dirichlet process paper.
- [Probabilistic Machine Learning: Advanced Topics](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262048439/probabilistic-machine-learning)  
  Broad modern reference connecting hierarchical models, latent variables, graphical models, and advanced Bayesian machine learning.

### Part V. Optimization, Tuning, and Diagnostics

- [Practical Bayesian Model Evaluation Using Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation and WAIC](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11222-016-9696-4)  
  Standard predictive model evaluation reference.
- [Rank-Normalization, Folding, and Localization: An Improved R-hat](https://doi.org/10.1214/20-BA1221)  
  Modern convergence diagnostic reference.
- [Visualization in Bayesian Workflow](https://doi.org/10.1111/rssa.12378)  
  Strong workflow paper connecting computation diagnostics and model criticism.

### Part VI. Software Workflows

- [R Project](https://www.r-project.org/)  
  Core language environment for the `R` side of the course.
- [Stan](https://mc-stan.org/)  
  Official Stan ecosystem site.
- [CmdStanR](https://mc-stan.org/cmdstanr/)  
  Official `R` interface used in the course.
- [posterior](https://mc-stan.org/posterior/)  
  Posterior-draw manipulation and summary tools.
- [bayesplot](https://mc-stan.org/bayesplot/)  
  Diagnostic and posterior visualization package.
- [loo](https://mc-stan.org/loo/)  
  Official package for PSIS-LOO and related diagnostics.
- [JAX](https://github.com/google/jax)  
  Autodiff and array programming system used for custom Bayesian computation.
- [NumPyro](https://num.pyro.ai/en/stable/)  
  Useful adjacent probabilistic programming system in the JAX ecosystem.
- [Quarto](https://quarto.org/)  
  Publishing system used to build this course.

### Part VII. Advanced Topics

- [The Bayesian Choice](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/0-387-71599-1)  
  Strong reference for Bayesian evidence, Bayes factors, and model comparison logic.
- [Using Stacking to Average Bayesian Predictive Distributions](https://doi.org/10.1214/17-BA1091)  
  Canonical stacking reference.
- [Projective Inference in High-Dimensional Problems](https://doi.org/10.1214/20-EJS1711)  
  Projection predictive model selection reference.
- [Bayesian Experimental Design: A Review](https://doi.org/10.1214/ss/1177009939)  
  Classic overview of Bayesian design criteria and methods.

## Core Texts

- [Bayesian Data Analysis](https://www.routledge.com/Bayesian-Data-Analysis-Third-Edition/Gelman-Carlin-Stern-Dunson-Vehtari-Rubin/p/book/9781439840955)
- [Monte Carlo Statistical Methods](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-1-4757-4145-2)
- [Probabilistic Machine Learning: Advanced Topics](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262048439/probabilistic-machine-learning)

## Canonical Textbooks

The most important book-length references for the course are:

- @bernardo1994
- @berger1985
- @gelman2013
- @hoff2009
- @robert2007
- @robert2004
- @brooks2011
- @mcelreath2020
- @mackay2003
- @murphy2023

## Landmark Papers And Articles

Core methodological papers repeatedly used in the course include:

- @metropolis1953
- @hastings1970
- @geman1984
- @andrieu2003
- @neal2011
- @hoffman2014
- @betancourt2017
- @kucukelbir2017
- @blei2017
- @vehtari2017
- @vehtari2021
- @gabry2019
- @ferguson1973
- @neal2000

## Software References

When the course discusses tools or packages, the preferred citations are:

- @r2025
- @carpenter2017
- @cmdstanr2024
- @posterior2024
- @bayesplot2024
- @loo2024
- @jax2018
- @numpyro2020

## Recommended Reading Paths

### Foundations-first

- @durrett2019
- @casella2002
- @bernardo1994
- @jeffreys1961

### Applied modeling

- @gelman2013
- @hoff2009
- @mcelreath2020
- @gabry2019

### Computation-heavy

- @robert2004
- @brooks2011
- @betancourt2017
- @hoffman2014
- @blei2017

## Full Bibliography
